title: System Administrator
description: **About Air Apps** At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger—and moving faster. We’re a family\-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI\-powered Personal \& Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal in 2018—and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco—we’ve remained self\-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide. Our long\-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI\-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe. Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way. **The Role** As a **Systems Administrator** at Air Apps, you will be responsible for **managing and maintaining corporate IT infrastructure, user access, and SaaS tools** to ensure a secure and efficient work environment. You will oversee **device management, network security, access control, and IT support** for employees across the organization. This role requires **technical expertise in IT systems, cloud\-based tools, and security best practices** to optimize and scale internal operations. * This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast\-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts. **Responsibilities** * Manage and maintain corporate IT infrastructure, including SaaS applications, cloud services, and internal systems. * Oversee user access, account provisioning, and identity management across platforms (Google Workspace, Okta, Azure AD, etc.). * Ensure network security, endpoint protection, and data encryption policies are in place. * Monitor and optimize hardware and software performance, troubleshooting issues as needed. * Implement and enforce IT security best practices, including multi\-factor authentication (MFA) and role\-based access control (RBAC). * Maintain and support company\-wide SaaS tools (Slack, Zoom, Notion, Atlassian, etc.). * Assist in onboarding and offboarding employees by setting up and deactivating accounts. * Automate IT operations, workflows, and system monitoring to improve efficiency. * Maintain software licenses, compliance requirements, and IT documentation. * Provide technical support and training to employees, ensuring smooth system usage. **Requirements** * Around 3\+ years of experience in IT administration, systems administration, or IT support. * Experience managing cloud\-based corporate IT environments (Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, AWS, or Azure). * Proficiency in identity and access management (IAM) tools (Okta, Azure AD, JumpCloud). * Knowledge of endpoint management solutions (Jamf, Intune, Kandji, or similar MDM platforms). * Familiarity with network security, VPNs, firewalls, and zero\-trust security models. * Experience with IT automation and scripting (Bash, PowerShell, Python) is a plus. * Ability to troubleshoot hardware, software, and connectivity issues efficiently. * Strong understanding of IT compliance, data privacy regulations (GDPR, SOC 2\), and best practices. * Excellent problem\-solving skills and ability to work in a fast\-paced, remote\-first environment. **What benefits are we offering?** * Apple hardware ecosystem for work. * Annual Bonus * Top\-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind. * Transportation Budget to support your commute needs. * Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well\-being, and more. * Childcare support. * Air Conference \- an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together. * Pension Fund to support your long\-term financial planning. * Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active. * Meals 100% free at the hub. **Diversity \& Inclusion** At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger. **Application Disclaimer** At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI\-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification.

title: Workday Business Analyst
description: **\*\*We are open to this being fixed\-term employment or day rate contract. The project is for 6 months.\*\*** **About the role** The Business Analyst role will support optimisation of our Workday platform and delivery of Workday's Advanced Compensation module through improved processes, configuration, and governance across HR, Reward, and technical teams. This is a 6\-month fixed\-term contract reporting into our HR Master Data Lead. The role can be performed remotely or in one of our office locations (hybrid options available). **Key Responsibilities** * Supporting Workday optimisation initiatives, identifying opportunities to streamline functionality and improve user experience. * Facilitating workshops, process mapping, and requirements gathering with HR, Reward and business stakeholders. * Translating business requirements into Workday functional specifications, user stories, and acceptance criteria. * Implementing automated solutions that resolve or reduce manual processes and align with Workday best practice. * Supporting the creation, embedding and maintenance of HR data governance, including data definitions, controls, data quality frameworks and change governance. * Developing and maintaining process documentation, including process flows, SOPs, configuration guides and change logs. * Supporting UAT planning and execution, including test script creation and defect management. **About you** * Proven hands‑on experience with Workday HCM, including configuration, tenant review and troubleshooting. * Experience implementing or enhancing Workday Advanced Compensation module (merit, bonus, stock). * Strong capability in process improvement and understanding end‑to‑end HR process flows. * Skilled at working with technical teams to ensure accurate configuration, integration and reporting outcomes. * Understanding of HR processes, including Core HR, Talent, Compensation and data governance. * Ability to translate business needs into clear functional specifications. * Strong documentation, analytical and problem‑solving skills. **What Success Looks Like** * Optimised Workday processes that deliver a smoother, more intuitive experience for HR and the business. * Well defined, clearly articulated requirements and documentation that support high‑quality system changes. * A successful rollout of Advanced Compensation, with engaged stakeholders and minimal defects. * Strong governance in place, with clear data definitions, quality controls and change processes. * HR teams confident in new processes and functionality due to effective training and knowledge transfer. * Continuous improvement embedded, with feedback loops that drive ongoing Workday enhancements. **About Inizio** At Inizio, we're a global team driven by a shared purpose: to reimagine health. Formed in 2022 through the coming together of Ashfield and Huntsworth, we unite decades of experience with cutting\-edge capabilities to deliver impactful solutions across the commercialization journey \- from discovery to post\-launch. Our global network of over 10,000 experts operates across 50 countries, supporting clients with strategic insight, medical excellence, creative engagement, and real\-world delivery. We work with the world's leading health and life sciences businesses to solve complex challenges and drive meaningful outcomes for our clients and patients. What sets Inizio apart is our people \- and we believe everyone should have the opportunity to thrive. Here, you're encouraged to reimagine your possibilities: to grow, to be curious and to make real change happen. It's a place where your individuality is valued, your development is supported, and your ambition is celebrated. If you're ready to push boundaries and accelerate your career, Inizio is the best place to be. **Our Pledge** At Inizio, we value inclusivity, recognize the power of diversity and inspire the next generation of change\-makers. We are an equal opportunities employer. We believe in creating a dynamic work environment that values diversity, equity and inclusion. We aim to recruit from a diverse slate of candidates and foster an environment that provides the right conditions for long\-term success. We welcome all applications regardless of race, color, religion or belief, gender, gender identity, age, national origin, marital status, military veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation and physical or mental disability. *Don't meet every job requirement? That's okay! Our company is dedicated to building an inclusive and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role, but your experience doesn't perfectly fit every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this role or others.*
